data.title: Intel declines for third straight day as Citi assesses Gelsinger departure, options
data.description:
Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) shares fell nearly 5% on Thursday — their third straight day of declines — as investors continued to wonder about the future of the semiconductor giant following Pat Gelsinger's departure.

Citi analyst Christopher Danely, who has a Neutral rating and $22 price target on Intel, said whoever takes over the top spot at the Santa Clara, Calif.-based company needs three qualifications: "advanced manufacturing experience, technical acumen, and Intel/CPU experience."

While the early names that have already been bandied about in the press — including former Intel board member Lip Bu Tan and Marvell (MRVL) Chief Executive Mark Murphy — do not have the requisite experience, Danely posited Intel could go back to the proverbial well for the top spot. (Murphy seemed to refute the rumors and said this week that he was "all in" on Marvell.)

"We believe former Intel executive Gregory Bryant could be a decent fit given his Intel experience and some technical acumen," Danely said.

Bryant, who is currently an executive at Analog Devices (ADI), worked at Intel for nearly a decade in a variety of roles, including leading Intel's largest segment, its Client Computing Group.

Despite that experience, Danely still believes that Intel should exit the foundry space, which was a pillar of Gelsinger's strategy for trying to turn the company around.

"If Intel exits foundry, gross margins could rise to the low to mid 50% range and EPS in the $3.00-$4.00 range which could translate to a $50-$60 stock," Danely wrote.

David Zisner — who is one of Intel's interim CEOs, along with Michelle Johnston Holthaus — told attendees of this week's UBS investment conference that Intel's core strategy remains "intact" and it will stay a company focused on products and foundry.

Separately on Thursday, Intel announced that former ASML (ASML) executive Eric Meurice and Microchip (MCHP) Chairman and interim CEO Steve Sanghi to its board of directors.

data.title: Novo Nordisk slashes U.S. list prices for insulin meds Tresiba, Fiasp
data.description:
• Novo Nordisk said it will cut the list prices in the U.S. of two of its insulin drugs, Tresiba (insulin degludec) and Fiasp (insulin aspart), by more than 70% starting Jan. 1, 2026.
  • Fiasp's price will be reduced by 75%, making it the same price as NovoLog (insuin aspart), while Tresiba's price will be reduced by 72%, resuting in it costing 20% less than its unbranded biologic equivalent.
  • As a result, Novo will also discontinue the manufacture of unbranded biologic versions of insulin aspart and insulin degludec.
  • Novo said it is notifiying doctors and payers a year in advance of the changes to allow patients to transition.

data.title: Condor Resources announces life offering private placement of up to $1.5M
data.description:
• Condor Resources (OTCPK:CNRIF) on Thursday announced a non-brokered private placement of up to 10M units of the company at a price of $0.15 per unit for gross proceeds of up to $1.5M.
  • Each Unit will consist of one common share of the company and one-half of one non-transferable common share purchase warrant.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds raised from the LIFE offering for exploration at the Huinac Punta Project, the Pucamayo Project, and for general corporate purposes.
  • It is expected that closing of the LIFE offering will take place on or about December 13.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Mainstreet Equity reports FY2024 results
data.description:
• Mainstreet Equity (TSX:MEQ:CA) said in FY 2024, posted double-digit, year-over-year growth across all key operating metrics, with funds from operations before current income tax increasing 33%, FFO increasing 23%, net operating income rising 22% and rental revenues increasing 19%.
  • The company also achieved double-digit growth in Q4, with major gains in FFO (27%), NOI (24%) and rental revenues (20%).
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Mace Security International stockholder approves merger with W Electric Intermediate Holdings
data.description:
• Mace Security International (OTCQB:MACE) announced that the stockholders voted in favor of the agreement and plan of merger by and among W Electric Intermediate Holdings, Mace Merger Sub, and wholly-owned subsidiary of parent, Mace. 
  • The stockholders also voted in favor of appointing Charles A. Gaddis as substitute stockholders' representative pursuant to the terms of the merger agreement.
  • The closing of the merger is anticipated to take place on or about Friday, December 6, 2024.
  • MACE shares were up 1.4% on Thursday.  
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: BitFuFu owns 1.6K bitcoin
data.description:
• BitFuFu (NASDAQ:FUFU) said it owns 1,664 BTC as of December 5.
  • “We have been refining our BTC treasury strategy and have a dedicated team managing our BTC assets," CEO, Leo Lu, said.

data.title: Rio Tinto CEO Stausholm dismisses activist investor call to unify stock listing
data.description:
An activist shareholder's call for Rio Tinto (NYSE:RIO) to abandon its primary U.K. stock listing does not make economic sense, CEO Jakob Stausholm said Wednesday at the company's investor presentation in London.

"We have just not got anything that tells us that the structure we have is not the best structure," Stausholm said, according to Bloomberg. "There is a lot of value preserved in that structure."

Hedge fund Palliser Capital, which holds ~$250M of Rio (RIO) shares, said the miner's dual listing structure on the London Stock Exchange and Australian Securities Exchange has been an "unmitigated failure," leading to $50B in value destruction.

Jefferies analysts said the company should consider some sort of corporate restructure, believing "it is time for Rio to consider alternative structures including a possible breakup of the company to unlock value as the diversified mining model is a negative for Rio's valuation, in our view."

Rio (RIO) appears to be prioritizing development of its own copper resources over acquiring more copper production through dealmaking, Morgan Stanley analysts said following the investor briefing.

New copper chief Katie Jackson is focused on the ramp-up of Oyu Tolgoi and addressing the challenges at Kennecott, which "appears to take precedence over external M&A ambitions for now," the bank said.

data.title: Disney hikes annual cash dividend by 33% to $1.00/share
data.description:
• The Walt Disney (NYSE:DIS) declared a cash dividend of $1.00 per share. This represents a 33% increase over the $0.75 per share paid to shareholders during fiscal year 2024.
  • The dividend will be paid in two installments of $0.50/share semi-annually, vs. prior semi-annual dividend of $0.45.
  • (The company paid out a dividend of $0.30/share in January, and then $0.45/share in July, for a 2024 total of $0.75/share.)
  • Payable Jan. 16; to shareholders of record Dec. 16; ex-div Dec. 16.
  • Payable Jul. 23; to shareholders of record Jun. 24; ex-div Jun. 24.
  • See DIS Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.
